name: Kettlebell Biceps Curl
resistance_profile: The bell's mass hangs below and behind the hand, shifting the load line and keeping the grip and forearm heavily involved through the mid-range.
cues: Keep the wrist firm so the bell stays off the forearm
Control the bell on the descent
execution_steps: Hold a kettlebell in each hand by the handle so the bells hang and the weight sits behind the wrist.
Stand tall with elbows tucked to your sides and palms facing forward.
Curl the kettlebells toward your shoulders, keeping the wrists firm so the bells do not crash into your forearms.
Pause briefly at the top, then lower under control to full elbow extension.
good_when: Training in a kettlebell-only or minimalist setup
You want extra grip and forearm involvement
You want a slightly different resistance feel than dumbbells
bad_when: The offset bell bangs your forearm and disrupts the set
You want a clean, even resistance curve
common_errors: Letting the bell bang the wrist or forearm at the top
Swinging the bells up with hip drive
progressions: Curl one arm at a time to increase load per side
regressions: Perform seated on a bench to remove momentum
